---
title: "Mete Akcaoglu"
---

# Mete Akcaoglu, Ph.D.

Professor of Instructional Technology · Department Chair, Leadership, Technology,
and Human Development · Georgia Southern University, College of Education ·
Savannah, GA 31419

📞 912.478.0002 · [makcaoglu@georgiasouthern.edu](mailto:makcaoglu@georgiasouthern.edu) · [meteakcaoglu.com](https://meteakcaoglu.com)

## About

I am a professor of instructional technology and department chair at Georgia
Southern University. My research focuses on how people learn with and through
technology — with particular emphasis on motivation, curiosity, and the design
of technology-rich learning environments.

I study game-based learning, interest development, and AI in educational settings.
My work spans experimental, survey, and qualitative methods.

[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=0y1b0oEAAAAJ&hl=en) ·
[GitHub](https://github.com/meteakca) ·
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/meteakcaoglu/) ·
[Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/meteakca.bsky.social)

## Research Interests

- Interest development, and motivation in technology-rich learning environments
- Game-based learning and computational thinking in K–12
- AI in education and LLM-assisted research workflows
- Online learning design and community of inquiry

## Current Position

**Department Chair — Leadership, Technology, and Human Development**
Georgia Southern University · Fall 2024–Present

Overseeing 20 full-time faculty, 10 graduate programs, ~600 students. Focus
areas: faculty development, curriculum reform, enrollment management, and
accreditation.

## News

- **2026** — Co-authored *Computational Analysis of Educational Data: A Field Guide Using R* with Wang, Rosenberg, & Kellogg. [Read online →](https://data-edu.github.io/computational-social-science-cookbook/)
- **2025–2026** — Co-PI on Math Nation Georgia Pilot Program Evaluation ($1M, Georgia Department of Education)
- **2024** — Keynote at the International Symposium on Computer and Instructional Technologies (ICITS), Kastamonu, Türkiye
- **2024** — Appointed Department Chair, Leadership, Technology, and Human Development
- **2022** — Completed NSF-funded Project GAME: a game design-based CS curriculum for middle schools
